title: The Cursed Forest
description: A lone traveler pushes into a forest no one has ever returned from. Whispers, shadows, and a choice that will change everything.
author: Terminal Stories

text: The trees close behind you like jaws.
Daylight vanishes within a few steps. Here, it is always dusk.
A sign half-eaten by time lies on the ground: 'MIREVAL FOREST, DANGER, TURN BACK'.
Too late to turn back. You decided that before you ever set out.

choices.text: Follow the main trail north
choices.action: You set foot on the old stone trail, barely visible beneath the moss.
choices.consequence: The path feels familiar... as if someone had prepared it for you.

choices.text: Explore the edge of the woods before going deeper
choices.action: You cautiously follow the treeline, scanning the shadows.
choices.consequence: A pale glow flickers in the distance, among the roots of an old oak.

choices.text: Call out to announce your presence
choices.action: You shout your name into the leafy dark.
choices.consequence: Silence. Then an echo... but it isn't your voice that answers.

text: The voice comes from everywhere at once. Deep, ancient, like wood cracking under snow.
The branches above you slowly interlace, forming something that resembles a face.

choices.text: "Who are you? Show yourself!"
choices.action: You draw your blade, rust and all.
choices.consequence: A laugh murmurs through every leaf. "Fear makes men brave. Or stupid."

choices.text: "I only seek passage. I want no trouble."
choices.action: You sheathe your blade and open your hands, palms forward.
choices.consequence: A long silence. Then: "Few have known how to ask. Follow the water."

text: The trail winds between ancient oaks whose roots have devoured the old flagstones.
Luminescent mushrooms grow in perfect circles. A bad sign.
Around a bend, you find a cabin. Smoke curls from the chimney.

choices.text: Knock on the cabin door
choices.action: You rap three sharp knocks on the old wood.
choices.consequence: A quavering voice: "Come in, traveler. The tea is hot."

choices.text: Skirt around the cabin and keep going
choices.action: You slip quietly behind the cabin.
choices.consequence: Something watches you from the window. You don't turn around.

text: The glow comes from an insect the size of your fist. A giant firefly, with translucent wings.
It flits toward you, hesitates, then heads off to the northeast.
Creatures like this don't exist in ordinary forests.

choices.text: Follow the firefly
choices.action: You follow it through the thorny brush.
choices.consequence: It guides you to a stream of black water that sings softly.

choices.text: Ignore the firefly and find your own way
choices.action: You push into the woods without a guide.
choices.consequence: The forest swallows you. You walk for what feels like hours.

text: The inside is cluttered with dried herbs, vials, and books whose titles read backward.
The old woman hands you a cup without looking at you. Her eyes are entirely white.

text.speaker: old-weaver
text.text: "Sit down. I'll tell you what you came looking for, and what you stand to lose."

choices.text: "I'm listening."
choices.action: You sit and take the cup. It smells of pine and sulfur.
choices.consequence: She speaks for a long time. You learn the location of the forest's heart, and its guardian.

choices.text: "I need nothing. Just passage."
choices.action: You back toward the door.
choices.consequence: She smiles for the first time. "That's what all the ones who never come back say."

text: The water is black as ink but perfectly clear. You can see the bottom, coins, bones, shattered mirrors.
On the far side of the stream, the forest changes. The trees are white. Long dead.
An ancient wooden bridge spans the water. On each pillar, a face is carved.

choices.text: Cross the bridge
choices.action: You step onto the bridge. The planks creak with every step.
choices.consequence: The faces turn their stone eyes toward you. They're waiting for something.

choices.text: Follow the stream to its source
choices.action: You follow the current north.
choices.consequence: The source is a hollow tree whose trunk weeps a black sap.

text: The trees draw closer. The ground heaves. Roots surge up like arms.
The Spirit takes shape, not a being, but a form: leaves, shadow, and wind interwoven.

text.speaker: spirit
text.text: "You wanted to see me? Here I am, little human."

choices.text: Run for your life
choices.action: You run without looking back.
choices.consequence: The forest lets you flee. For now.

choices.text: Hold your ground and drive your blade into the earth
choices.action: You plunge your blade into the soil. A gesture of defiance, or of offering.
choices.consequence: The Spirit stops. "Interesting."

text: You walk. And walk again. The forest repeats itself in a loop.
The same twisted tree. The same moss-covered stone. The same crow watching you.
Exhaustion sets in. Night falls, or maybe it's always night here.

choices.text: Lie down and sleep
choices.action: You rest your head on the roots of a tree.
choices.consequence: Dreams show you a path. When you wake, the ground has changed.

choices.text: Keep going no matter what
choices.action: You walk in a straight line, refusing to let yourself be turned around.
choices.consequence: After an hour, you stumble into the black stream.

text.speaker: old-weaver
text.text: "The heart of the forest is a single tree, white as bone, old as memory."

text.speaker: old-weaver
text.text: "It guards something. Something that belongs to you, or belonged to someone you loved."

text: She lays a hand on your arm. Her skin is cold.
She holds out a small vial filled with golden light.

choices.text: Take the vial and thank her
choices.action: You slip the vial carefully into your pocket.
choices.consequence: The light warms against your skin. You know now where to go.

text: The forest grows denser. The ground disappears beneath a carpet of tangled roots.
Sounds follow you, not animals. More like breaths. Presences.
At last you reach a clearing. At its center: a white tree, colossal, its branches touching the low clouds.

choices.text: Approach the white tree
choices.action: You cross the clearing slowly.
choices.consequence: Something stirs within the bark.

text: The hollow tree is immense. Three men could not encircle it.
The black sap running from its trunk forms words in the earth: 'REMEMBER'.
Inside the hollow, something glows with a light you recognize.

choices.text: Step into the hollow of the tree
choices.action: You slip inside.
choices.consequence: The space is impossible, far larger within. You find yourself facing the heart of the forest.

text: The Spirit crouches. An almost human shape.

text.speaker: spirit
text.text: "You didn't run. Men always run. Tell me, what do you truly seek?"

text: For the first time since you entered the forest, the wind stops.

choices.text: Tell the truth about what you seek
choices.action: You speak. The words come out on their own, things you hadn't said in a long time.
choices.consequence: The Spirit listens. "Follow the stream. I will be behind you."

choices.text: Lie, invent a noble quest
choices.action: You make up a story about a royal mission.
choices.consequence: "Trees do not lie. They tell me everything." A long silence. "Go. But without my help."

text: The white tree pulses like a heart. Its bark is covered in faces, hundreds of them.
You recognize some of them.
A voice comes from the tree itself. Or perhaps from your memory.

text.speaker: spirit
text.text: "You've arrived. Now, choose: take what belongs to you, or let the forest keep its secrets."

choices.text: Reach for the light within the tree
choices.action: Your hand touches the bark. It is warm.
choices.consequence: A memory returns to you, complete, painful, true.

choices.text: Turn back and leave the forest to its secrets
choices.action: You step back slowly, eyes still on the tree.
choices.consequence: The tree seems... relieved. A path opens between the roots.

choices.text: [VIAL] Pour the golden vial over the roots
choices.action: You uncork the vial and let the light spill into the earth.
choices.consequence: The tree lights up entirely. The faces in the bark smile.

text: You remember everything.
Why you came. What you lost. What you did.
The forest lets you go, but you are no longer the one who entered.
Sometimes, cursed forests only keep what men refuse to face.
— ENDING: The Weight of Memory —
You leave the forest at dawn.
The sign is behind you. The sun ahead.
You didn't find what you were looking for. But you refused to lose yourself to get it.
Sometimes, coming back alive is the greatest victory.
— ENDING: The Wisdom of Letting Go —
The light spreads into every root, every branch, every leaf.
The faces in the bark fade away, freed.
The Spirit of the Forest lets out a sigh as long as a century.

text.speaker: spirit
text.text: "It has been a long time since anyone knew how to give without taking."

text: The Mireval Forest is no longer cursed.
And you, you go home, the vial empty but your heart full.
— ENDING: The Gift Given —
